applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented a fused Triton kernel that combines subtract, hardswish, maxpool2d, and mish operations into a single GPU kernel. Key implementation details: (1) Used tl.math.exp and tl.math.log instead of tl.libdevice functions which are unavailable on ROCm, (2) Implemented manual tanh calculation for mish since tl.tanh is unavailable on ROCm, (3) Used BLOCK_SIZE=512 with vectorized processing where each program handles 512 elements in parallel using tl.arange, (4) Proper grid sizing using triton.cdiv to avoid exceeding hardware grid dimension limits.

attempted_but_failed.name: One thread per element with large grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Grid size (65M) exceeded hardware limits, causing incorrect results for high PIDs

attempted_but_failed.name: Loop-based processing with BLOCK_SIZE
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break/continue statements in loops

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on PyTorch baseline
attempted_but_failed.reason: Compilation errors with ttg.async_copy_global_to_local on ROCm

top_bottlenecks: Elementwise operations (subtract, hardswish, mish) fused into single kernel
MaxPool2d 2x2 reduction fused into elementwise kernel

notes: ROCm Triton constraints: tl.tanh, tl.libdevice.* are unavailable - must use manual implementations
Grid size must be limited - use triton.cdiv(n_elements, BLOCK_SIZE) not n_elements directly
Achieved 1.33x speedup (3.32ms vs 4.43ms reference) with correct Triton kernel

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Only ~29 minutes remain and the agent has failed to produce any output in 3 consecutive trials (trials 2, 3, 4). The best score of 63.40 (1.33x speedup) was achieved in trial 1 with a fused Triton ker
